author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
That Some Men are Elected is of God’s Mercy.
title: That Some Men are Elected is of God’s Mercy.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Chapter 11 [VI.]—That Some Men are Elected is of God’s Mercy.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: “Many hear the word of truth; but some believe, while others contradict.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Therefore, the former will to believe; the latter do not will.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Who does not know this?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Who can deny this?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But since in some the will is prepared by the Lord, in others it is not prepared, we must assuredly be able to distinguish what comes from God’s mercy, and what from His judgment.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: “What Israel sought for,” says the apostle, “he hath not obtained, but the election hath obtained it; and the rest were blinded, as it is written, God gave to them the spirit of compunction,—eyes that they should not see, and ears that they should not hear, even to this day.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And David said, Let their table be made a snare, a retribution, and a stumblingblock to them; let their eyes be darkened, that they may not see; and bow down their back always.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Here is mercy and judgment,—mercy towards the election which has obtained the righteousness of God, but judgment to the rest which have been blinded.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And yet the former, because they willed, believed; the latter, because they did not will believed not.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Therefore mercy and judgment were manifested in the very wills themselves.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Certainly such an election is of grace, not at all of merits.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For he had before said, “So, therefore, even at this present time, the remnant has been saved by the election of grace.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And if by grace, now it is no more of works; otherwise grace is no more grace.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Therefore the election obtained what it obtained gratuitously; there preceded none of those things which they might first give, and it should be given to them again.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: He saved them for nothing.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But to the rest who were blinded, as is there plainly declared, it was done in recompense.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: “All the paths of the Lord are mercy and truth.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But His ways are unsearchable.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Therefore the mercy by which He freely delivers, and the truth by which He righteously judges, are equally unsearchable.
